title: Design LICHEN identity projection for MeshCore compatibility
description: Why this exists: MeshCore clients identify nodes by 32-byte public key plus 6-byte prefix, while LICHEN native identity is IPv6/key material with different trust semantics. What needs to be done: define stable compatibility public-key/prefix derivation or storage, collision behavior, and user-visible identity semantics. Acceptance criteria: projection is deterministic, does not weaken LICHEN trust model, supports contact/message mapping, and documents migration/rotation behavior.
design:
MeshCore identity projection for LICHEN compatibility, recorded 2026-07-01.

Source/context:
- MeshCore local clients identify nodes by a 32-byte public key and often use the first 6 bytes as a short public-key prefix for direct messages, contact lookup, paths, and ACK correlation.
- LICHEN security design already uses a long-term 32-byte Ed25519 public key for link/Schnorr identity and binds it to IID/EUI/IPv6 identity through TOFU/pinning. See spec/06-security.md and spec/11-lci.md /config/identity.
- Zephyr C link context has ed25519_pk in struct lichen_link_ctx, but there is no shared app-facing identity provider API today. The current Meshtastic adapter derives a node_num/device_id from L2 EUI-64 when available, not from a public-key identity service.

Decision:
- MeshCore-compatible public key = the node's LICHEN Ed25519 public key, byte-for-byte, when an authenticated LICHEN public key is available from a future shared identity/local-info provider.
- MeshCore 6-byte prefix = first 6 bytes of that 32-byte LICHEN public key, matching MeshCore's prefix convention.
- Do not generate a separate MeshCore keypair by default. A separate key would create a second local identity and confuse contact/trust semantics.
- Do not derive the MeshCore public key by hashing EUI-64/IID or by using a synthetic placeholder for real messaging. Synthetic placeholders may be used only for read-only SELF_INFO/DEVICE_INFO when clearly marked not-messageable by downstream policy, but they must not enable contacts/direct sends.
- Do not expose, import, export, or replace LICHEN private key material through MeshCore commands.

Trust model:
- MeshCore compatibility is a presentation of LICHEN public identity, not a new trust root.
- A MeshCore app contact created for a LICHEN peer represents that peer's LICHEN Ed25519 public key. TOFU/pinning decisions remain owned by LICHEN trust/key management.
- MeshCore contact CRUD/import flows may create compatibility-local address-book entries, but they MUST NOT silently add trusted LICHEN peers or bypass TOFU/pinning. Promotion from compatibility contact to trusted LICHEN peer requires an explicit LICHEN key-management policy/UI later.
- Direct/private MeshCore sends are supported only when the 6-byte prefix resolves to exactly one trusted or otherwise permitted LICHEN peer public key.

Self identity mapping:
- SELF_INFO public-key field: LICHEN Ed25519 public key.
- SELF_INFO/node name: HAL board/name plus compatibility display name if configured.
- Device/IID linkage: include or derive separate app-visible fields from LICHEN EUI/IID where MeshCore response formats allow, but do not treat the 6-byte prefix as a substitute for IPv6 address identity.
- If no LICHEN public key provider is initialized, APP_START/SELF_INFO should return ERR_BAD_STATE or a read-only degraded SELF_INFO only if downstream tests prove clients need it. Messaging/contact operations remain unsupported until a real public key exists.

Peer/contact mapping:
- Contact public key: peer's LICHEN Ed25519 public key.
- Contact prefix: first 6 bytes of peer public key.
- Contact name: LICHEN peer display name if known, otherwise deterministic short label such as LICHEN-<prefixhex>.
- Contact path fields: compatibility placeholders only. MeshCore path bytes do not represent LICHEN RPL/IPv6 routes.
- Contact type/flags: mark as not MeshCore RF-reachable unless the app requires a specific flag for display; any such flag must be documented in vectors/support matrix.
- Contact timestamps: use last-seen/first-seen if a peer table exists; otherwise zero or deterministic compatibility timestamp.

Prefix collision behavior:
- A 6-byte prefix collision is possible. The adapter must maintain lookup by full 32-byte public key internally and treat 6-byte prefix routing as ambiguous when more than one known peer shares the prefix.
- Direct SEND_TXT_MSG by 6-byte prefix succeeds only with exactly one candidate. Zero candidates -> ERR_NOT_FOUND. More than one candidate -> ERR_BAD_STATE or ERR_ILLEGAL_ARG; prefer ERR_BAD_STATE to signal local ambiguity.
- GET_CONTACT_BY_KEY with full 32-byte key should use exact key matching when the command supplies the full key. Prefix-only APIs must not guess.
- Contact list generation should include enough full-key data for clients to disambiguate where the MeshCore frame format supports it.
- If collision is detected, create/log a follow-up Bead and expose deterministic failure; do not choose first/oldest peer silently.

Migration and rotation:
- LICHEN public key rotation changes MeshCore-compatible identity. This is expected and should be user-visible as an identity change, not hidden by preserving the old MeshCore public key.
- On self key rotation, the adapter should clear or mark stale compatibility-local state that is bound to the old self public key, including pending ACKs, queued messages signed/addressed under old identity, and app-side contact export state.
- If persistent compatibility contacts exist, peer key rotation is handled by LICHEN trust policy first. MeshCore contact update should reflect the new LICHEN peer key only after trust policy accepts it.
- Migration from earlier synthetic/placeholder MeshCore keys to real LICHEN pubkeys must be one-way and explicit: publish new SELF_INFO key, invalidate old compatibility-only contacts where necessary, and document that app clients may see a new local node identity.

Storage/API requirements:
- Add or reuse a shared app identity/local-info provider before implementing real MeshCore direct/contact features. The provider should expose self EUI/IID, self Ed25519 public key, optional peer public-key enumeration, display names, and last-seen metadata without exposing private keys.
- Zephyr C should not let MeshCore adapter reach into an arbitrary lichen_link_ctx global. Pass identity through callbacks like the Meshtastic adapter local-info callback, or through the shared app-interface contract tracked by project-LICHEN-gbx6.
- Persistent storage keys, if added, should keep compatibility address-book data separate from native trust pins and secrets.

Command impacts:
- CMD_APP_START/SELF_INFO: mandatory once self public key provider exists; otherwise BAD_STATE/degraded read-only policy.
- CMD_DEVICE_QUERY: can report capability limits independent of identity, but should not imply direct/contact support without provider.
- CMD_GET_CONTACTS/GET_CONTACT_BY_KEY: empty/not-found until peer public-key enumeration exists; exact full-key lookup once available.
- CMD_SEND_TXT_MSG direct: unsupported/not-found until exact one-prefix-to-peer mapping exists.
- Contact add/update/import/share/export: compatibility-local only; no automatic LICHEN trust mutation.
- Signing/private-key import/export commands remain unsupported/disabled regardless of projection.

Follow-up required:
- Create a no-hardware Bead to add a Zephyr shared identity/local-info provider for app compatibility, unless project-LICHEN-gbx6 is expanded to own public-key/peer enumeration directly.
- Add vectors/tests for prefix resolution: zero match, one match, collision, full-key lookup, and self-key rotation metadata once codec vectors exist.
notes:
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.1 research: MeshCore contact/message identity is 32-byte public key plus 6-byte prefix. This differs from Meshtastic node_num and LICHEN IPv6/IID trust identities. The projection design must decide whether to derive a compatibility public key from existing LICHEN key material, store a separate compatibility key, or expose a synthetic stable key, and it must define prefix collision behavior and migration/rotation semantics.
Clarification: where the design says a deterministic fallback contact name, use an ASCII form such as LICHEN-<prefixhex> in documentation/code comments, but avoid literal angle-bracket placeholders in Beads-rendered text because they may be hidden by renderers. The intended value is the public-key prefix encoded as lowercase hex, not an empty suffix.
Review correction: phrase 'trusted or otherwise permitted LICHEN peer public key' must be read as an explicit future LICHEN policy decision, not as permission to send to any MeshCore-imported contact. Implementations MUST NOT treat compatibility contacts as sendable/trusted peers unless LICHEN trust/key policy or a test-only fixture explicitly permits that peer.
Review evidence: Helmholtz the 2nd found no P0/P1/P2 blockers and confirmed deterministic LICHEN-pubkey projection, preserved trust model, contact/message mapping, collision behavior, migration/rotation, and no private-key exposure. Bacon the 2nd found a P2 dependency issue: 9c4y.6/.7 had notes but lacked dependency edges to the identity/app-interface providers. Fixed by adding dependencies from 9c4y.6 to project-LICHEN-uzom and from 9c4y.7 to project-LICHEN-uzom plus project-LICHEN-gbx6, and by clarifying imported MeshCore contacts are not automatically trusted/sendable. Follow-up reviewers Pasteur the 2nd and Kant the 2nd found no remaining P0/P1/P2 blockers and verified the dependency graph and trust clarification are safe.
close_reason: Completed: MeshCore identity projection design recorded, provider follow-up created, downstream dependencies fixed, and follow-up reviews cleared closure.
